title: Chromolaena molina
taxonomy: Chromolaena molina is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the tropical sage or devil's nettle. The genus Chromolaena is a group of flowering plants that are native to the Americas and the Caribbean. The family Asteraceae, also known as the daisy or sunflower family, is one of the largest and most diverse plant families in the world. Chromolaena molina is a member of the subfamily Asteroideae, which includes many common garden flowers and weeds. This classification system helps to understand the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of the plant.
